title: Dubai Chambers, Texmas discuss sustainable textile sector growth
content:
Dubai Chambers recently discussed with the Textile Merchants Group (Texmas) ways to strengthen the textile sector’s readiness to adapt to global developments and enhance its capacity for sustainable growth. 
Their meeting underlined the importance of reinforcing Dubai’s position as a leading global hub for textile trade. 
Participants reviewed the current landscape and future outlook for the sector.

title: South India notices mixed trend in cotton yarn, prices up in Tiruppur
content:
South India’s cotton yarn market showed mixed trends, with prices in Tiruppur rising by ₹2–3/kg (~$0.02–$0.04/kg) due to higher cotton costs, while Mumbai remains steady amid weak demand. 
Mills face difficulty passing on cost increases as buying stays cautious. 
Lower arrivals and higher CCI prices are supporting cotton rates, tightening supply ahead of lean months.

title: Top Vietnamese, Chinese leaders hold talks on advancing cooperation
content:
China and Vietnam are accelerating efforts to navigate bilateral trade towards a more balanced and sustainable course. 
President Xi Jinping recently held talks with visiting Vietnamese President To Lam. 
During the visit, Vietnamese Minister of Industry and Trade Le Manh Hung called for a restructuring of production, trade and supply chains alongside stronger investment cooperation.

title: Italy’s OVS’ FY25 sales rise 7% to $2.06 bn; beats market
content:
OVS has posted record FY25 sales of €1,745.9 million (~$2.06 billion), up 7 per cent YoY, driven by like-for-like growth and Goldenpoint consolidation. 
Adjusted gross margin rose 8.8 per cent, while net profit increased 14.8 per cent. 
Key brands delivered solid EBITDA gains. 
Womenswear and beauty led growth, with early FY26 performance remaining strong on robust collection demand.
